match_approach: Troy Titans sit 13th in Group B with 3 wins and 6 losses from 9 games (NRR -1.09). Their negative NRR signals both batting and bowling struggles. This is a must-win fixture for WW. Bat first if you win the toss — set a target of 110+ and let Ajinkya Pradhan and Yuvraj Rajpurohit defend it. Contain their top-order early and the game should be won by the 12th over.

batting_order.name: Vinit Keni
batting_order.reason: Team's leading run-scorer (174 runs, 34.80 avg). Reads pace well and builds the platform in the powerplay.

batting_order.name: Vivek Singh
batting_order.reason: Captain with an explosive 129.58 SR. Gets the innings moving quickly alongside Vinit.

batting_order.name: Vedant Kumar
batting_order.reason: Most experienced batter by matches (9 games, 97 runs). Steadies the innings if an early wicket falls.

batting_order.name: Malav Shah
batting_order.reason: Highest SR in the top 7 (146.67). Accelerates from over 6 onwards and punishes any width.

batting_order.name: Yogen Phanse
batting_order.reason: Solid 105.36 SR with 59 runs this season. Provides the middle-overs push.

batting_order.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
batting_order.reason: Vice Captain. 80 runs with the bat and our leading wicket-taker. Manages his energy here — bat smartly, bowl full quota.

batting_order.name: Anurag Dabee
batting_order.reason: 52 runs across 8 matches. Reliable lower-order anchor.

batting_order.name: Siddhant Kumar
batting_order.reason: Chips in with 28 runs and 6 wickets. Useful dual-role late in the innings.

batting_order.name: Tejas Bawaskar
batting_order.reason: 40 runs in 4 games — capable of a quick cameo at the end.

batting_order.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
batting_order.reason: Key bowler (11 wickets). Bats late to preserve energy for bowling duties.

batting_order.name: Abhinav Tyagi
batting_order.reason: Pure bowling asset (7 wickets). Bat aggressively if wickets in hand — nothing to lose.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Best economy in the squad (3.60 across 34 overs). Opens with him to set the tone and dry up runs early.

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.reason: Team's best economy figure (2.83). Extremely tight in the powerplay — generates dot-ball pressure.

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.reason: 4.05 economy over 20 overs. Reliable containment option through the middle.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: 4.53 economy and 11 wickets. Dangerous through the middle — will create pressure.

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.reason: 6 wickets at 6.73 econ. Variation bowler — useful to break partnerships.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Non-negotiable at the death. His slower ball and yorker make him our best death bowler by a distance.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: 11 wickets this season. Backs up Pradhan at death and handles pressure situations well.

bowlers.name: Abhinav Tyagi
bowlers.reason: Raw pace (7 wickets) as a wildcard at the death if needed.

key_matchups: Ajinkya Pradhan must bowl his full 4-over quota — 17 wickets at 3.60 economy is match-winning quality against a struggling Troy Titans batting lineup.
Open the batting with Vinit Keni and Vivek Singh to put pressure on Troy Titans' bowlers early. A 50-run powerplay would set up an imposing total.
Namit Rathi in the powerplay is the surprise card — his 2.83 economy will frustrate any aggressive opener Troy Titans field.
Malav Shah bats at No. 4 with licence to swing from ball one. His 146.67 SR is the highest in WW's top 7 and should accelerate the innings from over 8 onwards.
